news.title: Dalmandi drive gains pace, 112 buildings demolished so far in Varanasi
news.para: Demolition for the Dalmandi road-widening project has resumed, with over 112 structures razed to improve access to Kashi Vishwanath Dham. The project, aiming to widen the 650-meter road to 17.4 meters, has seen over 90 buildings acquired and compensation exceeding ₹50 crore disbursed. Negotiations continue for six mosques in the project's path.

news.title: Why India’s $105 billion GCC opportunity is a turning point for real estate
news.para: India's Global Capability Centres are transforming into innovation hubs, driving significant growth in the real estate sector.  Projected to reach $100-110 billion by 2030, these centres are demanding new cities, formats, and higher quality commercial and residential spaces, reshaping India's urban landscape.

news.title: Odisha housing board set to expand affordable housing with four projects
news.para: Odisha State Housing Board plans four major projects for FY 2026-27, aiming to deliver nearly 6,877 inclusive residential units. These include the Suango PPP project, Ranasinghpur EPC housing, Patrapada housing, and Basanti Colony joint venture. The board is also seeking government land for affordable housing initiatives to address urban housing needs.

news.title: Gurugram: Two missing links force seven km detour to e-way for Vatika commuters
news.para: Thousands of families in Gurugram's Vatika India Next township face connectivity woes due to incomplete sector road sections, a decade after its launch. The missing links, stemming from land acquisition delays, force a 7km detour to the Dwarka Expressway and worsen monsoon waterlogging. Residents demand urgent resolution.

news.title: Suraj Estate's subsidiary acquires development rights for land in Mumbai for ₹75 crore
news.para: Suraj Estate Developers is expanding its Mumbai presence. The company has secured development rights for a prime plot in Mahim, Mumbai. This acquisition is set to significantly boost its commercial project, Suraj One Business Bay. The new development is projected to add substantial saleable area and a considerable gross development value. This strategic move enhances the project's overall worth.

news.title: Puravankara to jointly develop 11.23-acre land in North Bengaluru
news.para: Puravankara has secured an 11.23-acre land parcel in Doddagubbi, North Bengaluru, for a residential project estimated at ₹1,100 crore. This move aligns with the company's robust FY26 performance, which saw sales surge by 55% to ₹7,407 crore. Aiming for ₹11,200 crore in FY27 sales, Puravankara is strategically expanding in high-demand markets.

news.title: NCLAT offers Supreme Housing final chance to settle Canara Bank dues
news.para: Supreme Housing promoters have a final opportunity to settle their debt with Canara Bank. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has ordered a deposit of ₹460 crore by June 13. This is the third chance for the company to resolve its financial obligations. Failure to meet this deadline will allow the insolvency process to proceed.

news.title: Lokayukta cops bust massive illegal sand mining network in Shivamogga
news.para: Karnataka Lokayukta police conducted a covert, two-month operation, utilizing satellite tracking to raid an extensive illegal sand-mining network across Shivamogga district. The meticulously planned action, bypassing local authorities, seized 17,000 metric tonnes of sand, 52 vehicles, and machinery, exposing deep-rooted collusion among officials.

news.title: Buyers can’t regularise illegal construction merely because they invested in it: Calcutta HC
news.para: The Calcutta High Court has upheld the demolition of a building's fourth floor under the Howrah Municipal Corporation. The court ruled that flat buyers cannot demand regularization of unauthorized construction simply because they invested in it. Owners who purchased the top floor were informed that their claim of title was not supported by evidence of authorized construction.

news.title: Former Sanghi Industries promoter settles dispute with Ambuja Cements, withdraws NCLAT plea
news.para: Alok Sanghi withdrew his appeal against Ambuja Cements at the NCLAT. This follows a settlement regarding Sanghi's Rs 84 crore personal guarantee. Ambuja Cements, which acquired Sanghi Industries, also withdrew its plea against Sanghi. The litigation between the two parties has now concluded. This brings an end to the legal battle initiated over the personal guarantee.

news.title: ED to record statements of Jaipur farmers alleging land grab by developers
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has summoned farmers in Jaipur as part of a money laundering probe into alleged fraudulent land aggregation. Farmers claim their ancestral land was taken through misused powers of attorney by real estate developers, despite legal sales and full payment. The agency is examining ownership records and powers of attorney from the past two decades.

news.title: Godrej Properties wins auction for 23.2 acres residential land parcel in Greater Noida
news.para: Godrej Properties has secured a 23.2-acre residential land parcel in Greater Noida's DMIC integrated township, aiming for a ₹7,000 crore revenue potential from a new group housing project. This acquisition follows their recent ₹1,100 crore partnership with Tata Projects for construction on three Gurugram projects.

news.title: With 283 ‘dangerous’ buildings, Ulhasnagar braces for rain fury
news.para: Ulhasnagar Municipal Corporation's pre-monsoon survey identified 283 dangerous buildings, with six declared extremely hazardous and requiring immediate evacuation. A significant number of structures need major or urgent repairs, and 144 lack essential structural audit reports, raising concerns about their stability as monsoon approaches.

news.title: Chandigarh administration may allow industrial plot fragmentation for four kanal units
news.para: Chandigarh's UT administration has proposed allowing fragmentation of industrial plots measuring 4 kanal and above, with a maximum of three subdivisions and a minimum plot width of 50 feet. This move aims to ensure regulated development, preventing colony-like layouts and mandating adequate parking. Revised zoning plans will be required before construction.

news.title: Chennai's town planning department to issue building permits within 27 days
news.para: Chennai's town planning department will now issue building permits within 27 days, aiming to curb corruption and delays. Officials are barred from summoning applicants, with all processes, including payments and clarifications, moving online. This move ensures time-bound, transparent service delivery for the public, streamlining approvals for various building types.

news.title: May 31 last date to pay property tax, avail rebate: Chandigarh civic body
news.para: Property owners in Chandigarh have a final chance to save money on their taxes. The municipal corporation is urging everyone to pay by May 31 to secure a 20% rebate on residential and 10% on commercial property tax. Failure to meet this deadline will result in a hefty 25% penalty plus 12% interest.

news.title: Mumbai property registrations rise 7% to 12,315 units in May 2026: Knight Frank
news.para: Mumbai recorded 12,315 property registrations in May 2026, up seven per cent year-on-year, marking the highest registrations for the month of May in 14 years, according to Knight Frank India. Stamp duty collections stood at ₹1,051 crore during the month, marginally lower than the year-ago period.

news.title: Irregularities in Solan housing project under Himachal Pradesh HC scanner
news.para: Himachal Pradesh High Court has issued notices to state and central governments, CBI, and ED. This is regarding alleged illegalities in the Chester Hills housing project. The project is on nearly 275 bighas of agricultural land. Allegations involve violations of land transfer laws. A report highlighted concerns about non-agriculturist promoters' control.

news.title: MahaRERA dues recovery slows down despite dedicated officers
news.para: Despite appointing dedicated officers five months ago, Maharashtra's initiative to recover dues for homebuyers has yielded only a meager Rs10 crore increase. MahaRERA has issued recovery warrants worth nearly Rs792 crore since 2017, but actual collections remain sluggish, leaving many homebuyers waiting years for compensation.

news.title: ED arrests promoter of Noida-based Bhasin Infotech and Infrastructure
news.para: Satinder Singh Bhasin, promoter of a Noida realty firm, has been arrested by the Enforcement Directorate. The arrest is in connection with a money laundering case. This case involves allegations of duping home buyers. Bhasin was taken into custody on Friday. He was sent to ED custody until June 6. The Supreme Court had directed the ED to arrest him.
